content: In C:\Users\mathm\Documents\Projects\Tuff\compiler\index.test.ts, add test case executeTuff("let x = 0U8; let y = &x;") => 0

[Session context summary — replaces earlier history]
The user is developing a recursive descent interpreter/compiler in TypeScript at `compiler/index.ts`, subject to strict ESLint constraints requiring functions under 250 lines. Recent work focused on control flow, type inference, and bare integer literals defaulting to I32. The immediate goal was implementing function definitions (`fn get() : I32 => ...`) and calls (`get()`).

In `compiler/index.ts`, a `FuncDef` interface (returnType, bodyStartPos) was added, and the `ParserCtx` interface was updated to include a `functions: Map<string, FuncDef>`. The tokenizer was extended to handle the `=>` operator. A new helper function `executeFunctionCall` was created to save context position, jump to the stored function body start index, execute the expression via `parseExprWithType`, and restore the original position.

The `parseBlockItem` function was modified to detect `fn` declarations; it parses the signature (handling tokenizer quirks where parentheses may attach to identifiers like `"get("`), stores the definition in context, and skips past body tokens using an existing helper. The `parseTermWithType` function was updated to check if a parsed identifier is followed by `(`; if so, it triggers `executeFunctionCall`.

In `compiler/index.test.ts`, two test cases were addressed:
1.  `"let x = 0U8; let y = &x;" => 0`: This already existed (twice) and passed after fixing a TypeScript error where the context initialization in `executeTuff` was missing the required `functions` map property.
2.  `"fn get() : I32 => 100; get()" => 100`: Added as requested to verify function definition and invocation logic.

Several syntax errors (unexpected closing braces) occurred during iterative edits of large code blocks in `parseBlockItem`, which were resolved by rewriting the sections cleanly. The final test run shows all 57 tests passing, including the new function call implementation.
Got it, I have the prior context.

Good progress! The function declaration parsing works, but the function call is failing because `get(` (with attached paren) doesn't match as a valid identifier in `parseTermWithType`. Let me check how identifiers with trailing parens are handled there:

I see the issue. The tokenizer produces `"get("` as a single token for function calls like `get()`. So when we reach line 601, the regex `/^[a-zA-Z_]\w*$/` doesn't match `"get("` because of the trailing paren. I need to handle this case:

I see the issue — my edit accidentally removed the final `else` branch that handles literal tokens (like `"100U8"`). When none of the conditions match, `result` stays undefined and everything crashes. Let me fix this by restoring the missing else clause for literals:
